name: Jaylin Lane
headline: Deep-shot rookie flash, but a crowded room caps the ceiling
body:
Lane's rookie tape reads like a vertical specialist who never got a real on-ramp: 16 grabs on 32 targets across 15 games, with 13.3 air yards per look and a 26.7 air-yards-per-catch number that screams downfield usage. The problem is everything underneath it. A 36.2% snap share, 7.4% target share, and a -0.177 EPA per play with a 40.6% success rate point to a low-volume role where the offense bet on splash plays rather than steady work. Three catches of 20-plus and a 41-yard long show the juice; two interceptions thrown his way and a 50% catch rate show the volatility.

The 2026 path is narrow. An ankle injury that landed him on injured reserve clouds the offseason, and Washington's receiver room now includes Chig Okonkwo, rookie Antonio Williams, and Treylon Burks all chasing the same targets. Until Lane wins a defined route tree beyond punt-return duty, he's a waiver-wire stash, not a draft-day asset.
